Example: go-linear attachment create --issue=ENG-123 --title="Build #42" --url=https://ci.example.com/42 --metadata='{"status":"passed"}' --output=json

description:
Create issue. Safe operation.

Required: --team (name/key from team_list), --title
Optional: --description, --assignee=me or email, --priority (0=none, 1=urgent, 2=high, 3=normal, 4=low), --state, --label (repeatable)

Example: go-linear issue create --team=ENG --title="Fix bug" --assignee=me --priority=1 --description="Details" --output=json

Related: issue_get, issue_list, team_list, user_list, label_list

args.description: Positional command line arguments

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

name: go-linear_issue_create

description:
⚠️ Delete issue. Cannot be undone. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes.

Example: go-linear issue delete ENG-123

Related: issue_list, issue_get

args.description:
Positional command line arguments
Usage pattern: <id>

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

name: go-linear_issue_delete

description:
Get issue by identifier (e.g., ENG-123) or UUID. Returns 10 default fields.

Examples:
  go-linear issue get ENG-123 --output=json
  go-linear issue get ENG-123 --fields=defaults,estimate --output=json
  go-linear issue get ENG-123 --fields=id,title,url --output=json

Related: linear issue list (discover IDs), linear issue update

args.description:
Positional command line arguments
Usage pattern: <id>

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

name: go-linear_issue_get

description:
List issues with filtering. Returns 8 default fields per issue. Use --count for totals (99% fewer tokens).

Filters: --team (name/key), --assignee=me (current user) or email, --state (name), --priority (0-4), --label (name), --created-after=yesterday|7d|2w|2025-12-10

Pagination: --limit (default 50), --after (cursor from pageInfo.endCursor)
Count: --count returns just {"count": N} instead of full results

Example: go-linear issue list --team=ENG --assignee=me --priority=1 --output=json

Returns: {nodes: [{8 issue fields}...], pageInfo: {hasNextPage, endCursor}} or {"count": N} with --count
Related: issue_get, issue_create, team_list, user_list

args.description: Positional command line arguments

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

count.description: Return only count, not results (99% token reduction)

name: go-linear_issue_list

description:
Relate two issues. Safe operation.

Types: blocks | blocked-by | duplicate | related (default)

Example: go-linear issue relate ENG-123 ENG-124 --type=blocks --output=json

args.description:
Positional command line arguments
Usage pattern: <issue-id> <related-issue-id>

description:
Display current rate limit quotas and usage.

Makes a lightweight API call (Viewer query) to fetch current rate limit headers.

Output shows:
  - Request-based limits (remaining / total)
  - Complexity-based limits (remaining / total)
  - Reset times

Examples:
  # Show status in table format
  go-linear status

  # JSON format for scripting
  go-linear status --output=json

description:
Get completed issues by user or team. Complex query - replaces 5-step workflow.

Filters: --user=me or email (single user) | --team (all team members), --completed-after=yesterday|7d (see issue_list), --completed-before=today
Must specify EITHER --user OR --team.

Example: go-linear user completed --team=ENG --completed-after=yesterday --completed-before=today --output=json

Returns: [{user: {name, email}, count: number}...]
Related: user_list, team_list, issue_list

args.description: Positional command line arguments

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

team.description: Team name or ID (queries all team members)

user.description: User name, email, or ID (or 'me' for current user)

name: go-linear_user_completed

Get user by name, email, 'me', or UUID. Returns 7 default fields.

Example: go-linear user get me --output=json

Related: user_list, user_completed

args.description:
Positional command line arguments
Usage pattern: <name|email|id>

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

name: go-linear_user_get

description:
List users. Returns 6 default fields per user. Use for discovering emails for --assignee.

Example: go-linear user list --output=json

Returns: {nodes: [{6 user fields}...], pageInfo: {hasNextPage, endCursor}}
Related: user_get, user_completed, issue_list

args.description: Positional command line arguments

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

name: go-linear_user_list

description:
Get information about the currently authenticated user.

Examples:
  go-linear viewer
  go-linear viewer --output=json

args.description: Positional command line arguments

api-key.description: Linear API key (or set LINEAR_API_KEY env var)

name: go-linear_viewer
